name: White Chocolate Coconut Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
2. Grease three 9-inch round cake pans and line the bottoms with wax paper, also greasing the paper.
3. Melt white chocolate and water in a double boiler over simmering water, stirring until smooth. Allow to cool.
4. In a large mixing bowl, cream butter and 1 1/2 cups sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in egg yolks one at a time, ensuring well-incorporated after each addition.
5. Sift together flour, baking powder, and salt. Alternately add the flour mixture and buttermilk with vanilla extract and melted white chocolate to the butter mixture, beating until smooth after each addition.
6. In a small mixing bowl, beat egg whites until soft peaks form. Gradually fold in the remaining 1/2 cup sugar, beating until stiff but not dry. Gently fold this into the batter along with nuts and coconut.
7. Divide batter evenly among prepared pans. Bake for 30 to 35 minutes, or until a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out clean. Allow to cool on wire racks for about 10 minutes, then remove from pans and let cool completely on racks. Remove wax paper.
8. For frosting, beat cream until it becomes frothy. Add confectioners' sugar and vanilla extract, beating until soft peaks form. Fold in flaked coconut. Place one cake layer onto a serving platter and frost. Repeat with remaining layers, then frost the top and sides of the cake. Refrigerate for up to 2 hours before serving.
